name: Embalse de Valdeinfierno from Vélez-Rubio — circular by Vélez-Blanco
name_original: Embalse de Valdeinfierno desde Vélez-Rubio — circular por Vélez-Blanco
region_intro: Touring cycling around Vélez-Rubio offers diverse landscapes within the Los Vélez region of Almería, Spain. The area features a mix of well-paved roads, gravel paths, and mountain trails, characterized by mountain landscapes, forests, and rural roads. Cyclists can explore protected natural areas like the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park, which presents varied microclimates and extensive pine forests, alongside challenging mountain ranges such as the Sierra de las Estancias. The region also includes historical castles and dramatic limestone peaks, providing varied terrain for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Vélez-Rubio The most popular touring cycling route is Santuario del Saliente and Chir, a 38.1 miles (61.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the region's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Sierra de las Estancias Loop from Vélez-Rubio, a difficult 23.1 miles (37.3 km) path. This route traverses diverse terrain within the Sierra de las Estancias mountain range, offering challenging climbs and scenic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Parque Natural Sierra de María-Los Vélez — circular desde Vélez-Rubio, a 41.8 miles (67.2 km) trail leading through the protected natural park with varied microclimates and pine forests, often completed in about 6 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Vélez-Rubio is defined by mountain landscapes, forests, and rural roads, with routes often featuring historical castles and dramatic limestone peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The routes in Vélez-Rubio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vélez-Rubio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Vélez-Rubio?
A: Vélez-Rubio offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 40 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Vélez-Rubio?
A: The region around Vélez-Rubio features diverse terrain, including well-paved roads, gravel paths, and mountain trails. You'll encounter mountain landscapes, forests, and rural roads, often with historical castles and dramatic limestone peaks providing scenic backdrops. Protected natural areas like the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park offer varied microclimates and extensive pine forests.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Vélez-Rubio?
A: The Vélez-Rubio region, being in Almería, Spain, generally offers favorable conditions for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, providing pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather than the peak summer months, which can be very hot. Winter can also be suitable, especially for those looking to escape colder climates.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites I can visit while cycling?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history and culture. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez-Blanco Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a majestic 16th-century Renaissance fortress, or explore the traditional whitewashed houses and the beautiful Iglesia de la Encarnación in Vélez-Rubio itself. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2580445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Saliente&lt;/a&gt; is another significant religious building in the vicinity.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on a touring cycling route?
A: Cyclists can enjoy stunning natural features such as the varied landscapes of the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park, characterized by extensive pine forests and diverse microclimates. You might also encounter natural water springs like the Fuente Caños de la Novia, known for its crystal-clear mountain water, and dramatic limestone peaks. For panoramic views, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2074254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sierra de María&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383506847/embalse-de-valdeinfierno-from-velez-rubio-circular-by-velez-blanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Valdeinfierno from Vélez-Rubio — circular by Vélez-Blanco&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 59.9 km route that takes you through varied landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez-Blanco – Vélez-Rubio loop from Vélez-Rubio&lt;/a&gt;, a 23.4 km difficult trail.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for beginners or less experienced touring cyclists?
A: While many routes in Vélez-Rubio are rated as difficult, there are 9 moderate routes available. These routes offer a good balance for those looking for a less strenuous ride while still enjoying the region's beauty. It's always recommended to check the route details, including distance and elevation gain, to ensure it matches your fitness level.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced touring cyclists will find plenty of challenging options. The region features 31 difficult routes, many with significant elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383521412/sierra-de-las-estancias-loop-from-ve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de las Estancias Loop from Vélez-Rubio&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 37.3 km path traversing diverse terrain with challenging climbs. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383527835/santuario-del-saliente-and-chir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Saliente and Chir&lt;/a&gt;, a 61.4 km trail with substantial elevation gain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Vélez-Rubio?
A: The touring cycling routes around Vélez-Rubio are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of well-paved roads and challenging mountain trails, and the stunning views of historical castles and natural parks like Sierra María-Los Vélez.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain and natural parks offer numerous scenic vistas. Cyclists can enjoy panoramic views of the agricultural valleys, lush pine-covered slopes, and dramatic limestone peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2074254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sierra de María&lt;/a&gt; is a notable viewpoint, and routes through the Sierra María-Los Vélez Natural Park often provide breathtaking scenery.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the cycling routes in Vélez-Rubio?
A: Vélez-Rubio and nearby towns like Vélez-Blanco offer various cafes and restaurants where you can refuel. While specific establishments along the routes are not detailed, the presence of charming historical towns means you'll likely find options for refreshments and meals within a reasonable distance of many trails.
tips:
Vélez-Rubio is a town that has amalgamated cultures over the centuries. Its urban center presents a very interesting architectural complex, mainly from the 18th and 19th centuries. Although some of its neighborhoods preserve the medieval spirit of the twisted and narrow streets.

On the other hand, its surroundings have a marked agricultural character dominated by olive groves.

Torre de Alacín or Montalviche is a watchtower from the 15th century. Although it is in a state of abandonment, it is worth taking a while to admire the landscape with the Sierra La Muela de Montelviche in the background.

Mountain pass, which exceeds 1000 m in altitude and separates the district of La Parroquia from the Valdeinfierno reservoir. Wide and perfect track for gravel. In dry seasons, a lot of dust/sand from the nearby quarries accumulates.
Located in the narrow gorge that the Luchena River excavates between the Gigante and Culebrina mountains. One of the most beautiful areas of the municipality of Lorca.

From Cantarerías Street in Vélez-Blanco, you can enjoy spectacular views of the Vélez-Blanco Castle. This street offers a unique perspective of the castle, allowing you to appreciate its imposing structure and strategic location on top of the hill.

Built in the 16th century, Vélez-Blanco Castle is one of the most outstanding monuments in the region. Its Renaissance architecture and fascinating history make it a must-see for visitors.
Built between 1506 and 1515 by order of Pedro Fajardo y Chacón, first Marquis of Vélez, Vélez-Blanco Castle is a fascinating destination for lovers of history and architecture.

waypoints.name: View of Vélez-Blanco and its castle
